cot_prefix: Okay, let me try to work through this problem step by step. The question is asking for the order of the places from east to west, given a description of a road from San Francisco to New York with businesses in order of size. The options are: - (A) San Francisco, Reno, New York, Columbus. - (B) San Francisco, Reno, Columbus, New York. - (C) New York, Columbus, Reno, San Francisco. - (D) New York, Reno, Columbus, San Francisco. First, I need to determine which cities are on each side of the U.S. In the U.S., San Francisco is on the west coast (California) and New York is on the east coast. So San Francisco would be the westernmost city and New York the easternmost. That already narrows down the options. Looking at the options: Option A: San Francisco (west) -> Reno -> New York (east) -> Columbus. But Columbus is in Ohio, which is in the central/eastern part of the U.S., so Columbus should be west of New York, but in this option Columbus is after New York, which is incorrect. Option B: San Francisco -> Reno -> Columbus -> New York. This seems plausible because San Francisco is west, then Reno (Nevada, which is also on the west coast but east of San Francisco), then Columbus (Ohio), then New York (on the east coast). Option C: New York (east) -> Columbus -> Reno -> San Francisco (west). This order is from east to west, but the question asks for the order from west to east, I think. But the question says "from east to west". Let me check. Actually, the question says "from east to west". So New York should be first (east), then Columbus, then Reno, then San Francisco (west).
